title: When Evaluating Actions: - Ask:
subtitle: Distinguish Intent from Accident
description:
When evaluating actions:
- Ask: "Could this have been an accident or unintentional?"
- Look for patterns: One mistake doesn’t indicate intent—watch for repeated behavior.
- Check your biases: Avoid assuming intent without solid evidence.

Example: If a coworker forgets to share notes, consider if it was an oversight rather than deliberate exclusion.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key metrics to track incidents and responses. Focus on the count of logged incidents and the minutes spent resolving them. This will help establish a baseline for evaluating patterns.

howTo.title: Log incidents consistently
howTo.summary: Create a habit of logging each incident with a brief description, context, and immediate impact. Use a two-line format to keep it simple and manageable.

howTo.title: Ask the key question
howTo.summary: When faced with a potential slight, pause and ask, 'Could this have been an accident or unintentional?' This helps slow down automatic assumptions and encourages evidence-based responses.

howTo.title: Review patterns over time
howTo.summary: After a set period, review logged incidents to identify any patterns. Use the threshold of three similar incidents in 14 days to determine if further action is needed.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app. Use the insights gained to adjust your approach and improve your understanding of intent versus accident. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/intent-vs-accident-coach/.
